item.dc:creator: dontlookinmybeard

item.title: Kiff, inform the men!
item.description:
Gentlemen, I am pleased to report that as I was stepping off the elevator, a cute lady walking her dog said, "I like your shirt!" I gave an enthusiastic "thank you" and continued on my way. Please join me in riding this high for the next ten years.
I'm walking for a bit, enjoying the shine, when...hold up. I am notoriously bad at picking up social signals. Was that a "hey, come talk to me" compliment or just a genuine "hey, I like your shirt?"
*inaudible anguish*
MV Edit: Woot! Thank you! No requests, I'm just happy to share something positive today. The shirt in question, for those wondering: https://i.imgur.com/g8hI0kh.png
item.dc:creator: shamrocksamurai

item.title: Meet Frank. You may not recognize him, but you know him.
item.description:
Frank, aka Frank Welker, turned 78 earlier this week. He's quite the accomplished voice actor, as you will soon see. All images below are characters that he has brought to life in dozens of studios.
Happy Belated Birthday, Frank.
item.dc:creator: MayMyEnemiesLiveLong

item.title: Does a bear dump in the woods? - 082
item.description:
Theeeeyyyyy'rrrrre late stage capitalism!
Magnificent, aren't they?
( ͡ʘ ͜ʖ ͡ʘ)
Do you like Wendy's?
Wendysnutz surge prices, haha, gottem
My favorite part of Halo: The Show was when John Halo said "it's haloin' time" and then haloed all over those aliens.
soda lactose
bork
THE TIDE RISES
Did we do it? Did we save the day light?

item.dc:creator: bearatrooper

item.title: The Middle-earth Halfling Marathon - Saturday 23 March 2024 !
item.description: The Middle-earth Halfling Marathon takes Halflings on a journey through sites and scenes from the epic trilogies including some locations previously only ventured to by wizards and never-before accessed by the public. Participants will roam through the picturesque village of Hobbiton, through Gandalf’s Cutting, past the Sackville Orchard and up to Bag End before weaving down to Bagshot Row and past the sparkling lakefront. If time allows, a stop for a brief refreshment at The Green Dragon Inn before the final leg of the adventure to the finish line across from The Shire’s Rest. https://www.hobbitontours.com/halflingmarathon/ I am not a time traveller people !!!!! The video you see here is posted on tiktok !!!!!! https://www.tiktok.com/@hobbitontours/video/7328192594322656530
item.dc:creator: JustaRandomCanadianFemale

item.title: At that point, if they are all a week long, your one week off cruises would be your vacation
item.dc:creator: tanijrou

item.title: Why Gandalf intimidated Bilbo
item.description:
This is a lore acocunt on Gandalf's reaction to Bilbo Baggins' accusation of deceitful envy; was it alone enough to set the Grey Wizard off?
In the book Bilbo had agreed to leave his ring behind, but after his party he stated that he would keep his "precious". The Grey recommended him to to let it go (The Fellowship of the Ring; I; I onwards): "`I’ll do as I choose and go as I please,’ said Bilbo obstinately. `Now, now, my dear hobbit! ‘ said Gandalf. ‘All your long life we have been friends, and you owe me something. Come! Do as you promised: give it up!‘
`Well, if you want my ring yourself, say so!’ cried Bilbo. ‘But you won’t get it. I won’t give my precious away, I tell you.’ His hand strayed to the hilt of his small sword. Gandalf’s eyes flashed. `It will be my turn to get angry soon,’ he said. ‘If you say that again, I shall. Then you will see Gandalf the Grey uncloaked.’ He took a step towards the hobbit, and he seemed to grow tall and menacing; his shadow filled the little room.
Bilbo backed away to the wall, breathing hard, his hand clutching at his pocket. They stood for a while facing one another, and the air of the room tingled. Gandalf’s eyes remained bent on the hobbit. Slowly his hands relaxed, and he began to tremble. `I don’t know what has come over you, Gandalf,’ he said. ‘You have never been like this before. What is it all about? It is mine isn’t it? I found it, and Gollum would have killed me, if I hadn’t kept it. I’m not a thief, whatever he said.’
`I have never called you one,’ Gandalf answered. ‘And I am not one either. I am not trying to rob you, but to help you. I wish you would trust me, as you used.’ He turned away, and the shadow passed. He seemed to dwindle again to an old grey man, bent and troubled." Bilbo apologized and despite some more inner struggle he left the ring, in an envelope on the mantelpiece for his nephew Frodo Baggins to find soon after.
Still, the confrontation caused Gandalf to finally seek the true nature of the ring, despite that his chief Saruman previously had decieved him that the One Ring was lost in the sea so that this could not be it... As for the topic, in conclusion it was not merely the slanderous accusation but that Bilbo was about to draw his sword, Sting, against his old friend that caused Gandalf to put the foot down and intimidate him.
Art credits: #2 Ruth Sanderson #3 SalReaper666 #4 DanielGovar #5 Donato Giancola — — — — — In case you enjoyed this and would like to read further Tolkien lore posts about characters, themes, objects and events, here are two indexes: Character bios https://imgur.com/gallery/axV2mPF Folks, animals, items, etc https://imgur.com/gallery/AQK9pJL
item.dc:creator: Ngugi

item.title: Poetry
item.description: I have been curating a poetry folder here on imgur, but have not posted any myself. I came across this one this morning and was struck by it. I have always been interested in poetry that touches on the reality of war, especially WWI poetry. From what I can find Marwan Makhoul is a Palestinian/Lebanese Poet who currently lives in Israel. Selections of his poetry have been translated into many languages.
item.dc:creator: FearTheZee
